title: NOC 72012 – Contractors and supervisors, pipefitting trades | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors in Pipefitting Trades (NOC 72012) are the technical leaders ensuring the integrity of Canada's essential fluid and gas transport systems. As the country focuses on "Net-Zero" building retrofits and the expansion of hydrogen and natural gas infrastructure, these professionals are in high demand to oversee the critical work of plumbers, steamfitters, and gas fitters. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they combine master-level trade knowledge with project management skills to ensure multi-million dollar mechanical systems meet the rigorous safety and environmental codes of 2026.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Project Supervision: Scheduling and coordinating the activities of plumbers, steamfitters, gas fitters, and sprinkler system installers.
Technical Troubleshooting: Resolving complex work problems and recommending measures to improve productivity and quality of mechanical systems.
Resource Logistics: Requisitioning materials and supplies and coordinating with other sub-trades to ensure project milestones are met.
Training & Safety: Mentoring apprentices and ensuring that all 2026 Safety Codes for high-pressure piping and gas installations are strictly followed.
Compliance Reporting: Preparing work progress reports and ensuring all installations comply with municipal and provincial building regulations.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson in a relevant trade (Plumber, Steamfitter, or Gas Fitter) are required.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in a relevant pipefitting trade is usually required.
Professional Skills: Proficiency in blueprint reading, CADD software, and advanced knowledge of automated flow-control systems.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, specifically driven by the National Housing Strategy and heavy industrial maintenance cycles:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On February 18, 2026, Ontario issued a targeted OINP draw specifically for NOC 72012 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. The minimum score required was 50, signaling a high priority for skilled pipefitting supervisors in the province's construction sector.
Alberta: Strong demand. Managers here earn the highest median wages in the country at $51.00/hr, supported by the energy sector and large-scale industrial fabrication in Edmonton and Fort McMurray.
British Columbia: Good prospects. Focused in the "Mainland/Southwest" region. BC projects nearly 3,000 openings in related construction trades through 2035, with median wages at $45.79/hr.

National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. With over 68% of the labor shortage in skilled trades driven by retirements, international recruitment is the primary solution for 2026–2033.
Why Work in Canada as a Pipefitting Supervisor?
Lead the Infrastructure Boom: Direct complex installations for high-density residential towers, hospitals, and industrial plants using 2026-grade CADD and Digital Flow Mapping tools.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your median annual earnings are approximately $81,123, with high-earners in provinces like Ontario reaching $65.48/hr.
Entrepreneurial Pathway: A high percentage of this NOC consists of Contractors who own and operate their own mechanical or plumbing firms.
Fast-Track to PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) draws, offering one of the most reliable routes to Permanent Residency.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On February 18, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72012 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is currently the most active pathway for this role.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly launched federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents with skilled trade experience. Pipefitting supervisors are considered "High-Priority Essential Workers."
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 technical trade, it is eligible for category-based draws for skilled trades, which typically have lower CRS requirements than general draws.
BC PNP Construction Stream: A high-success route for managers with a job offer from a BC-based mechanical or plumbing contracting firm.
